Look, salespeople stop sales, customers do not stop them. Okay? Now get this, your customer is never the problem. Never. You are the ultimate barrier to every sale. It is never, ever the customer. ## [03:00 - 05:17]

Look, you don't want to allow yourself this habit. It's a bad habit and you cannot afford this luxury. When you're not able to close a deal, make the sale, ask yourself, what did I not do? What did I miss? What am I not making or why am I not making the sale right now? What did I not uncover? What did I do wrong? Look, you could even ask these questions directly to the customer. You know, a customer once told me, hey, I'm not buying today and I said to him, so that would be my fault, not yours. I take full responsibility for your decision. This very seasoned professional buyer type looked at me and said, hey, that's good. I never heard that from a salesperson before. Within 60 minutes, the deal, the transaction was finished. See, I told him, it's true, sir. If something were not to work out today, it'd be my fault. I mean, come on, whose fault would it be? It's not his. You don't have to do anything, Mr. Customer. Nothing. You have to do nothing. Just tell me what you want, what you're trying to accomplish. I'll do everything else. So what I'm encouraging you to do, take responsibility for everything.
